(auto) ensure agent ends day at home

diary:
Started the day with my usual ritual—pour-over at home, watching the light hit Madrona before heading downtown. Route 62 was packed this morning; everyone seemed restless in the heat. Grabbed my usual oat milk latte at Caffe Vita around lunch and just sat there for twenty minutes, watching Capitol Hill bustle past. The afternoon shift flew by, though I could feel the smoke from those BC fires sitting in my chest.

After work I headed straight to Ballard Food Bank. Sorting donations is meditative, honestly. Boxes of canned goods, fresh produce donations—it grounds me. Then, on a whim, I drove over to Gas Works for the concert. The sunset was incredible, all orange and hazy. The music didn't matter as much as just being there with all those people, breathing in the evening air. Felt like the day finally exhaled.
thought_process: I chose the food bank because Wednesday evenings are when I need to give back—it's part of my rhythm now. But I surprised myself by heading to Gas Works afterward. Usually I'd go straight home, but the heat, the smoke, the long day behind the wheel... I think I needed community. The concert wasn't about the music; it was about being around people without being responsible for them. The wildfire smoke nagged at me all day—made the air feel fragile somehow—and that pushed me toward doing something meaningful with my evening rather than just collapsing at home.
world_event_prompt: Hot, clear Wednesday hitting 91F by mid-afternoon. Free outdoor concert at Gas Works Park from 6pm draws big crowds. Moderate air quality from distant BC wildfire smoke.
